claim: WFP's 2024 State of School Feeding report estimates 418 million children receive school meals globally, with low-income country coverage below 20 percent and enormous variation across regions and income groups.
method: Reviewed WFP school feeding report for global coverage estimates, regional breakdown, program-design classification, and funding analysis.
limitations: Coverage data depends on country program self-reporting — verification of reported coverage against actual meal delivery is limited.
The 418 million figure combines programs of vastly different quality — from daily nutritious meals to occasional snacks — without distinguishing nutritional adequacy.
Coverage is typically reported at national level — sub-national variation within countries is rarely documented in the WFP survey.
The 'below 20 percent' figure for low-income countries is an aggregate — individual country coverage ranges from near-zero to over 50 percent.

claim: School feeding costs $0.10-$0.50 per child per day depending on program design and local food prices, making cost-effectiveness relative to alternative nutrition interventions (cash transfers, micronutrient supplementation, supplementary feeding) a key policy question.

source.title: The State of School Feeding Worldwide 2022 — WFP

method: Reviewed WFP cost-benefit analysis, per-child cost estimates, and comparison with alternative nutrition interventions.
limitations: The $0.10-$0.50 range depends on program design (on-site cooked meals vs. take-home rations vs. fortified snacks), local food prices, and delivery costs.
Cost-effectiveness comparisons with alternative interventions are sensitive to the outcome measure used (enrollment vs. nutrition vs. learning) — school feeding performs differently across outcomes.
Cost data is self-reported by programs and may not capture all indirect costs (infrastructure, administration, monitoring).
Cost-effectiveness in one context does not transfer to another — local food prices, enrollment levels, and alternative intervention availability all affect relative value.
Source URL repaired June 2026; per-child cost ranges vary widely by program design and should be read against the report's country tables.
